name: 5-Minute Opening Range Breakout
description: Capture high/low of first 5 minutes after market open. Enter long on close above range high, short on close below range low. Most academically validated ORB window for US markets (Zarattini 2024, IEEE TORB 2019).

name: Dual Thrust Threshold Breakout
description: Calculate upper/lower thresholds from previous day OHLC (not just today's range). Entry when price exceeds threshold. Reversal when price crosses to opposite threshold. More adaptive than fixed-range ORB because thresholds incorporate prior day volatility.

name: Session Transition Breakout (London Breakout Pattern)
description: Establish range during one session's final hour, trade breakout in next session's open. Exploits information arbitrage across time zones. Adaptable to pre-market/RTH transition for MES.

name: 4-Phase State Machine Pullback Entry
description: SCANNING (6-layer validation: ATR, Angle, Price, Candle Direction, EMA Ordering, Time) -> ARMED (pullback confirmation 1-3 candles) -> WINDOW_OPEN (breakout window 1-20 bars with success/failure boundaries) -> ENTRY (ATR-based SL/TP). Prevents premature entries and provides systematic invalidation.

name: VWAP + 2 StdDev Band Touch (Mean Reversion)
description: Enter counter-trend when price touches or breaches VWAP +/- 2 standard deviation band. Long below lower band, short above upper band. Classic statistical mean reversion entry.

name: VWAP Band + OBV-RSI Dual Confirmation
description: Require BOTH price at VWAP deviation band AND OBV-RSI in oversold/overbought territory (<=30 or >=70). Dual confirmation dramatically reduces false signals by requiring both price AND volume-momentum extremes.

name: Z-Score Threshold Entry (Half-Life Calibrated)
description: Enter when z-score exceeds threshold (typically 1.8 StdDev). Window for standard deviation calculation calibrated to k * half-life of mean reversion (e.g., half-life=20 -> window=60-80). Prevents arbitrary parameter selection.

name: FVG Fill Entry (ICT)
description: Identify Fair Value Gap (bullish: prev high < next low; bearish: prev low > next high). Enter when price returns to fill the gap zone. Requires directional confirmation via BOS/CHoCH.

name: Order Block Retest Entry (ICT)
description: Identify Order Block (final accumulation/distribution candle before institutional move, measured by volume + 2 adjacent volumes). Enter on price retest of OB zone. OB strength measured by percentage metric.

name: Liquidity Sweep Entry (ICT)
description: Detect liquidity zones (multiple highs/lows clustered in small range). Enter after price sweeps through liquidity level and reverses. Liquidity detection returns level, direction, end index, and sweep candle index.

name: Triple Indicator Confirmation (VWAP + EMA Stack + RSI)
description: Require all three: price above/below VWAP (trend), EMA stack ordered (9>20>50 for long), RSI crossing 50. Enter on 9 EMA crossing VWAP with confirmation. High-conviction entry with low frequency.

name: Rolling Window Breakout (Multi-Period)
description: Long when price > N-bar high, short when price < N-bar low. Combine multiple window sizes (80, 160, 320 bars) for diversification. Signal smoothed with lookback = window/4. Sharpe ~0.77-0.79 per window.

name: ATR-Based Stop Loss (10% of 14-day ATR)
description: Stop loss placed at 10% of the 14-day ATR from entry price. Tight enough to limit losses, wide enough to avoid noise stops. Validated on 7000+ stocks (Zarattini 2024).

name: EOD Flat (Time-Based Close)
description: Close all positions at end of trading session regardless of P&L. Eliminates overnight risk. Standard for intraday ORB and mean reversion strategies.

name: VWAP Reversion Exit
description: Exit when price reverts to VWAP baseline after entering on deviation band touch. Primary profit target for mean reversion strategies. Can be combined with fixed stop loss (0.6% typical).

name: Asymmetric StdDev Exit
description: Enter at 1.8 StdDev, exit at -1.2 StdDev (for longs). Asymmetric thresholds capture most of the reversion move without waiting for full mean reversion. Based on Ernest Chan methodology.

name: 1.5x ATR Trailing Stop
description: Trailing stop set at 1.5 times the current ATR. Adapts to volatility. Conservative enough for prop-safe trading. Used in VWAP scalping and trend-following contexts.

name: Opposite Breakout Signal (Reversal Exit)
description: Exit (and optionally reverse) when price triggers the opposite breakout threshold. Used in Dual Thrust and N-bar breakout systems. Always in market - reduces missed moves but increases whipsaw risk.

name: State Machine Failure Boundary
description: If breakout window (1-20 bars) expires without hitting success boundary, reset to SCANNING state. No trade taken. Prevents forcing entries in ambiguous price action.

name: Fixed Stop + Mean Reversion Dual Exit
description: Combine hard stop loss (0.6% or fixed point distance) with VWAP reversion target. Whichever hits first determines exit. Protects against trend continuation while allowing reversion profit.

name: Stocks in Play / Abnormal Volume Filter
description: Only trade instruments showing abnormally high pre-market or early-session volume relative to average. This IS the edge in ORB - raw ORB without this filter shows 0.04% avg gain (essentially zero). For futures: use volume spike detection at session open.

name: Maximum Range Size Gate
description: No trade if opening range exceeds 0.8% of price. Prevents entering on already-volatile days where range has used up most of the daily move. Simple but effective volatility gate.

name: Day-of-Week Filter
description: Exclude specific days of the week that historically underperform for the strategy. Requires periodic recalibration. Simple edge that compounds over time.

name: 6-Layer Entry Validation (ATR/Angle/Price/Candle/EMA/Time)
description: Validate six conditions before arming entry: ATR within acceptable range, price slope angle sufficient, price at correct level, candle direction confirms, EMA ordering correct, time window valid. All six must pass.

name: OBV-RSI Extreme Filter (<=30 or >=70)
description: Only take mean reversion entries when OBV-RSI (RSI applied to On-Balance Volume) reaches extreme levels. Confirms that volume is supporting the reversion hypothesis, not just price.

name: CTI (Commodity Trend Index) < -0.8
description: Require CTI below -0.8 for long entries. CTI measures trend strength - values below -0.8 indicate countertrend exhaustion. Reduces false mean reversion signals in trending markets.

name: Stationarity Test (ADF) Pre-Filter
description: Run Augmented Dickey-Fuller test on price series before deploying mean reversion strategy. Only trade instruments/periods that pass stationarity test. Academically rigorous but computationally expensive for real-time.

name: BOS/CHoCH Directional Confirmation (ICT)
description: Require Break of Structure or Change of Character alignment before taking FVG/OB entries. BOS confirms existing trend continuation, CHoCH identifies trend reversal. Prevents fighting institutional flow.

name: Multi-Timeframe Structure Alignment (ICT)
description: Confirm higher-timeframe structure direction before taking lower-timeframe entries. Used in ICT 2022 model to ensure trades align with institutional order flow on daily/4H before entering on 15M/5M.

name: 5-Minute Close Confirmation (No Wick-Only Breaks)
description: Require a 5-minute candle CLOSE outside the range, not just a wick. Eliminates false breakouts from spike wicks. If first 5-min candle closes back inside range, no trade that day.

name: Daily Bar Filter (Higher Timeframe Trend)
description: Add one daily-timeframe condition to intraday ORB. Exact filter varies but dramatically improved raw ORB results from near-zero to tradeable edge per QuantifiedStrategies live trading.

name: NY Open Only (9:30-10:00 AM ET)
description: Establish opening range during first 5-30 minutes of NY session. Most volume and institutional activity occurs at open. Used by most ORB strategies.

name: London Open (3:00-3:30 AM ET / GMT 7:00-8:00 AM)
description: Last hour before London market open used as range-setting period. Breakout traded at London open. Captures Asian-to-London session transition volatility.

name: London Close (10:30 AM GMT)
description: ICT 2022 model secondary session. Institutional activity at London close creates FVG/OB setups. Used as alternative entry window when London Open doesn't trigger.

name: Full Intraday (RTH Only, EOD Flat)
description: Trade only during Regular Trading Hours. Mandatory flat by session close. Eliminates overnight gap risk. Standard for all prop trading strategies.

name: Multi-Session Detection (Sydney/Tokyo/London/NY)
description: Smart Money Concepts library can mark specific trading session boundaries. Enables session-aware strategies that behave differently across global sessions.
